steps.title: Simplify the inequality
steps.content:
Start by adding 18 to both sides to isolate the term with $x$:
$$ax - 18 \le 30$$
$$ax \le 48$$

steps.title: The solution form reveals the sign of a
steps.content:
The given solution $x \ge -8$ is a lower bound, not an upper bound. When dividing $ax \le 48$ by $a$, the sign of $a$ determines whether the inequality flips:
  • If $a \gt 0$: we get $x \le \frac{48}{a}$ (upper bound) - does not match
  • If $a \lt 0$: the inequality flips to $x \ge \frac{48}{a}$ (lower bound) - matches the required form
Therefore, $a$ must be negative.

steps.title: Solve for a from the boundary condition
steps.content:
Since dividing $ax \le 48$ by the negative number $a$ gives $x \ge \frac{48}{a}$, and this must equal $x \ge -8$, the boundaries must be equal:
$$\frac{48}{a} = -8$$
Multiply both sides by $a$:
$$48 = -8a$$
Divide both sides by $-8$:
$$a = \frac{48}{-8} = -6$$
The greatest possible value of $a$ is $-6$.
